1	//go:build !windows
2	
3	package agent
4	
5	import (
6		"os/exec"
7		"syscall"
8	)
9	
10	// cmdSetProcessGroup configures cmd so that it and all its children share a
11	// new process group (Setpgid) and are automatically killed when the parent
12	// thread dies (Pdeathsig). This ensures that if the parent worker dies
13	// abnormally (SIGKILL, crash, OOM), the harness child is reaped by the kernel
14	// rather than orphaning to PID 1.
15	//
16	// Pdeathsig is sent when the creating thread dies, not the process, so the
17	// caller must use runtime.LockOSThread around cmd.Start() to prevent Go
18	// runtime from migrating the goroutine to a different OS thread. See
19	// golang.org/issue/27505 for details.
20	//
21	// Note: Pdeathsig is Linux-only. macOS and Windows do not support it; the
22	// graceful shutdown path (cmdKillProcessGroup on context cancellation) serves
23	// as the fallback for those platforms, and the orphan reaper is the final
24	// backstop for already-dead workers.
25	func cmdSetProcessGroup(cmd *exec.Cmd) {
26		cmd.SysProcAttr = &syscall.SysProcAttr{
27			Setpgid:   true,
28			Pdeathsig: syscall.SIGKILL,
29		}
30	}
31	
32	// cmdKillProcessGroup sends SIGKILL to the entire process group of cmd.
33	// Because cmd was started with Setpgid=true, its PID equals its PGID, so
34	// passing -pid kills all descendants that inherited the group.
35	func cmdKillProcessGroup(cmd *exec.Cmd) {
36		if cmd.Process == nil {
37			return
38		}
39		_ = syscall.Kill(-cmd.Process.Pid, syscall.SIGKILL)
40	}
41

15	func TestDockerCloneBackend_ProcessGroupCleanup(t *testing.T) {
16		cmd := dockerAttemptCommand(context.Background(), "run", "--rm", "--init", "runner:latest")
17	
18		require.NotNil(t, cmd)
19		require.NotNil(t, cmd.SysProcAttr)
20		require.True(t, cmd.SysProcAttr.Setpgid, "docker-clone must start docker in its own process group")
21		require.Equal(t, syscall.SIGKILL, cmd.SysProcAttr.Pdeathsig)
22		require.Equal(t, "docker", filepath.Base(cmd.Path))
23		require.Contains(t, cmd.Args, "--rm")
24		require.Contains(t, cmd.Args, "--init")
25	}
26	
27	func TestDockerCloneBackendConfiguresProcessGroupForDockerCommand(t *testing.T) {
28		// Verify the docker-clone backend applies the same process-group setup contract
29		// to the docker invocation as other execute-bead harness paths.
30		cmd := dockerAttemptCommand(context.Background(), "ps")
31	
32		require.NotNil(t, cmd)
33		require.NotNil(t, cmd.SysProcAttr, "docker command must have SysProcAttr configured")
34		require.True(t, cmd.SysProcAttr.Setpgid, "docker command must set Setpgid for process-group isolation")
35		require.Equal(t, syscall.SIGKILL, cmd.SysProcAttr.Pdeathsig, "docker command must set Pdeathsig for parent-death cleanup")
36	}
37	
38	func TestDockerCloneBackendTerminatesContainerOnWorkerCancellation(t *testing.T) {
39		// Verify context cancellation or graceful worker shutdown stops the docker CLI process
40		// and the associated containerized harness tree.
41		// Note: This test verifies the mechanism without requiring an actual docker daemon.
42		// The actual container cleanup is guaranteed by the --rm flag in dockerRunArgs.
43	
44		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
45	
46		// Create a docker command that will be cancelled
47		cmd := dockerAttemptCommand(ctx, "ps")
48	
49		// Cancel the context to simulate worker shutdown
50		time.AfterFunc(10*time.Millisecond, cancel)
51	
52		// The command should respect context cancellation through exec.CommandContext
53		err := cmd.Run()
54	
55		// We expect an error since we cancelled the context
56		require.Error(t, err, "docker command should be interrupted by context cancellation")
57	
58		// Verify the context was actually cancelled
59		require.Eventually(t, func() bool {
60			return ctx.Err() != nil
61		}, 100*time.Millisecond, 5*time.Millisecond, "context should be cancelled")
62	}
63	
64	func TestDockerCloneBackendParentDeathCleanupDocumentedOrEnforced(t *testing.T) {
65		// Verify abnormal parent-death behavior for docker-clone is either enforced
66		// through parent-death/process-group/container cleanup or has a documented justification.
67	
68		cmd := dockerAttemptCommand(context.Background(), "ps")
69	
70		// Verify that process-group setup is in place for docker command
71		require.NotNil(t, cmd.SysProcAttr, "docker command must have SysProcAttr")
72		require.True(t, cmd.SysProcAttr.Setpgid, "Setpgid must be set for process-group isolation")
73		require.Equal(t, syscall.SIGKILL, cmd.SysProcAttr.Pdeathsig, "Pdeathsig must be set to SIGKILL for parent-death cleanup")
74	
75		// Verify the docker run arguments include --rm for automatic container cleanup
76		ws := &AttemptWorkspace{
77			ProjectRoot: "/repo",
78			WorkDir:     "/tmp/work",
79			BeadID:      "ddx-test",
80			AttemptID:   "test-attempt",
81			DockerRun:   "/tmp/runtime",
82			DockerHome:  "/tmp/runtime/home",
83		}
84		args := dockerRunArgs(nil, ws, "/usr/bin/ddx", "test:latest", nil)
85		require.Contains(t, args, "--rm", "docker run must include --rm for automatic container cleanup on exit")
86		require.Contains(t, args, "--init", "docker run must include --init for proper signal handling inside container")
87	}

func TestExecutorGracefulSignalStillTerminatesHarnessTree(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	childPIDFile := filepath.Join(dir, "child.pid")
	grandPIDFile := filepath.Join(dir, "grandchild.pid")
	scriptPath := filepath.Join(dir, "harness.sh")
	script := `#!/bin/sh
set -eu
sleep 300 &
echo $$ > "` + childPIDFile + `"
echo $! > "` + grandPIDFile + `"
wait
`
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(scriptPath, []byte(script), 0o755))

	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
	time.AfterFunc(100*time.Millisecond, cancel)

	executor := &OSExecutor{}
	result, err := executor.ExecuteInDir(ctx, scriptPath, nil, "", dir)
	require.Error(t, err, "graceful cancellation should stop the harness tree")
	require.NotNil(t, result)

	childPID := executorReadPIDFile(t, childPIDFile)
	grandPID := executorReadPIDFile(t, grandPIDFile)
	require.Eventually(t, func() bool {
		return !executorProcessAlive(childPID) && !executorProcessAlive(grandPID)
	}, 5*time.Second, 25*time.Millisecond, "graceful cancellation must kill the entire harness tree")
}

 	require.Contains(t, args, "--rm", "docker run must include --rm for automatic container cleanup on exit")
 	require.Contains(t, args, "--init", "docker run must include --init for proper signal handling inside container")
 }
+
+func TestDockerBuildCommandConfiguresProcessGroup(t *testing.T) {
+	// Verify that docker build commands spawned by the docker-clone backend
+	// are configured with process-group setup for proper cleanup.
+	cmd := dockerAttemptCommand(context.Background(), "build", "-t", "test:latest", ".")
+
+	require.NotNil(t, cmd.SysProcAttr, "docker build command must have SysProcAttr")
+	require.True(t, cmd.SysProcAttr.Setpgid, "docker build must set Setpgid")
+	require.Equal(t, syscall.SIGKILL, cmd.SysProcAttr.Pdeathsig, "docker build must set Pdeathsig")
+}
+
+func TestDockerImageInspectCommandConfiguresProcessGroup(t *testing.T) {
+	// Verify that docker image inspect commands are configured with process-group setup.
+	cmd := dockerAttemptCommand(context.Background(), "image", "inspect", "--format", "{{.Id}}", "test:latest")
+
+	require.NotNil(t, cmd.SysProcAttr, "docker image inspect command must have SysProcAttr")
+	require.True(t, cmd.SysProcAttr.Setpgid, "docker image inspect must set Setpgid")
+	require.Equal(t, syscall.SIGKILL, cmd.SysProcAttr.Pdeathsig, "docker image inspect must set Pdeathsig")
+}
+
+func TestDockerRemoveCommandConfiguresProcessGroup(t *testing.T) {
+	// Verify that docker rm commands are configured with process-group setup.
+	cmd := dockerAttemptCommand(context.Background(), "rm", "-f", "test-container")
+
+	require.NotNil(t, cmd.SysProcAttr, "docker rm command must have SysProcAttr")
+	require.True(t, cmd.SysProcAttr.Setpgid, "docker rm must set Setpgid")
+	require.Equal(t, syscall.SIGKILL, cmd.SysProcAttr.Pdeathsig, "docker rm must set Pdeathsig")
+}

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: ensure all docker commands in docker-clone backend use process-group cleanup [ddx-e582c37a]

Apply process-group setup (Setpgid + Pdeathsig) to all docker CLI invocations
in the docker-clone attempt backend, not just the main docker run command.
This ensures that if the worker is killed or cancels context:
- docker build for project images exits cleanly
- docker image inspect for image detection exits cleanly  
- docker rm for container cleanup exits cleanly

All docker commands now use dockerAttemptCommand() which configures the
process group, guaranteeing cleanup through executor process-group killing
or kernel parent-death signal handling.

Adds comprehensive tests to verify process-group setup for docker build,
docker image inspect, and docker rm commands.
EOF
)"

Apply process-group setup (Setpgid + Pdeathsig) to all docker CLI invocations
in the docker-clone attempt backend, not just the main docker run command.
This ensures that if the worker is killed or cancels context:
- docker build for project images exits cleanly
- docker image inspect for image detection exits cleanly  
- docker rm for container cleanup exits cleanly

All docker commands now use dockerAttemptCommand() which configures the
process group, guaranteeing cleanup through executor process-group killing
or kernel parent-death signal handling.

Adds comprehensive tests to verify process-group setup for docker build,
docker image inspect, and docker rm commands.
